steps.title: Find the core pivot
steps.content: The passage opens with a problem (“Having trouble getting rid of weeds?”) and immediately pivots to an unexpected solution (“One surprising solution may be to start eating them”). This problem-to-surprising-solution structure, with emphasis on the word “surprising,” defines the text's purpose.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content: The text proposes an unexpected solution to a problem. B, C, and D each misidentify the focus: gardening methods, taste as the main point, and trend identification. That matches choice A — A is correct.
